summary: Iteration 171: Default Message for Participants Feature - All tests PASS. Backend: (1) GET /api/user-settings/me/appointment-defaults returns default_message field (null by default), (2) PUT /api/user-settings/me with appointment_defaults.default_message saves correctly, (3) GET /api/user-settings/me returns default_message in appointment_defaults, (4) Max length 2000 chars validated. Frontend: (1) Profile page /settings/profile displays textarea 'Message pour les participants' with data-testid='profile-default-message', (2) Character counter X / 2000 displayed, (3) Save button works with success toast, (4) Value persists after page reload, (5) Wizard /appointments/create pre-fills description field with default_message when it exists.

backend.test_get_appointment_defaults_returns_de: PASS - default_message field present in GET /api/user-settings/me/appointment-defaults response
backend.test_save_default_message_via_put: PASS - PUT /api/user-settings/me with default_message saves and persists correctly
backend.test_default_message_returned_in_appoint: PASS - After saving, GET /api/user-settings/me/appointment-defaults returns the saved message
backend.test_default_message_can_be_cleared: PASS - default_message can be cleared by setting to empty string
backend.test_default_message_max_length_2000: PASS - default_message accepts up to 2000 characters
backend.test_get_user_settings_returns_default_m: PASS - GET /api/user-settings/me returns default_message in appointment_defaults after saving

frontend.default_message_textarea_present: PASS - Textarea with data-testid='profile-default-message' found and visible
frontend.character_counter_displayed: PASS - Character counter 'X / 2000' displayed below textarea
frontend.save_button_works: PASS - Save button (data-testid='save-profile-btn') works, success toast 'Paramètres enregistrés avec succès' appears
frontend.value_persists_after_reload: PASS - Saved default_message persists after page reload
frontend.wizard_loads_without_errors: PASS - /appointments/create wizard loads without JS errors
frontend.wizard_prefills_description: PASS - Description field (data-testid='appointment-description-input') pre-filled with default_message when it exists

critical_code_review_comments: user_settings.py L41: default_message field correctly defined as Optional[str] with max_length=2000
user_settings.py L78: Fallback value for default_message is None (correct)
user_settings.py L191: GET /api/user-settings/me/appointment-defaults returns default_message field
user_settings.py L110: exclude_none=True means sending null won't clear the field - must send empty string to clear
Profile.js L65: default_message correctly loaded from appointment_defaults
Profile.js L119: default_message correctly sent in handleSave with null fallback for empty
Profile.js L420-430: Textarea with data-testid='profile-default-message', maxLength=2000, character counter displayed
AppointmentWizard.js L232: Guard (!fromExternalData && !prev.description && defaults.default_message) correctly implemented for pre-fill priority
seed_data_creation: Test data created and cleaned up during tests (prefixed with TEST_ITER171_)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Default message for participants feature fully tested and working. Key implementation points: (1) Backend: default_message field in AppointmentDefaults model at L41 in user_settings.py, returned in both GET /me and GET /me/appointment-defaults endpoints, (2) Frontend Profile: textarea at L420-430 in Profile.js with data-testid='profile-default-message' and character counter, (3) Frontend Wizard: pre-fill logic at L232 in AppointmentWizard.js with guard (!fromExternalData && !prev.description && defaults.default_message). Note: To clear the message, send empty string (not null) due to exclude_none=True in backend.
